description_long: This object is a realistic representation of a shrimp or prawn, characterized by a segmented, elongated body with a hard exoskeleton. The body is divided into a cephalothorax and a flexible abdomen, with the cephalothorax bearing two prominent eyes on short stalks and long, slender antennae extending forward. The legs are thin and jointed, with the front pair slightly more robust, and the tail fans out at the end of the abdomen. The coloration is predominantly dark brown to gray, with subtle lighter banding along the segments and a slightly glossy, moist appearance typical of fresh crustaceans. The surface texture appears hard and smooth, with some fine ridges and details visible on the exoskeleton. This object is life-sized and would be found in food markets, restaurants, or as discarded food waste in urban environments.
description: A life-sized, dark brown shrimp or prawn with a segmented body, long antennae, and jointed legs, featuring a hard, glossy exoskeleton.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the shrimp, highlighting the segmented body, long antennae, and the curvature of the tail.
description_view_1: Top-down view displaying the symmetrical arrangement of legs, the width of the cephalothorax, and the spread of the tail fan.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, but with the antennae and legs oriented in the opposite direction.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail fan and the alignment of the legs, with antennae extending outward from the head.
